findings.message: ItemRead is the external API response schema and now exposes owner_email, which the model explicitly flags as PII ('do not include in logs or external responses'). Both GET /items and POST /items serialize through ItemRead, so owner email PII is echoed in external API responses to any caller.
findings.suggestion: Remove owner_email from ItemRead. If contact info must be returned, gate it behind an authorized/owner-only response schema rather than the default public list contract.

findings.message: ItemCreate collects owner_email (PII) from the request, but post_item calls create_item(session, payload.name) and never persists or uses the email. This is collection of PII not needed for the stated purpose; the value is silently accepted and dropped, which is both unnecessary collection and misleading.
findings.suggestion: Drop owner_email from ItemCreate unless it is actually required and will be stored with a defined retention/purpose. If it is required, persist it intentionally and document the purpose.
